reviews.review_title: I have been using it for DIY images
reviews.review_description:
I got the app to view 360 images I created 10 years ago using Google Cardboard Camera.   I had to modify the images to make them work but they did finally work.  

I am now using Pigasus to view 360 images I take with the camera on my Pixel phone. (The stock Panorama mode will go nearly 360 degrees)  Also 3D images I am taking using Scopi.

The reason I gave it only 4 stars is that it took a LOT of work to learn how to manipulate some of the images so they would show well.  When I finally got them to work it was 'mind blowing.'
I have tried several other Video Players (Both Free and Purchased) and this is the one that has given me the best video picture quality and real perpective 3D out of all of them.
Reads my local SMB share. Lots of video configurations. So far my best player on Quest 2.

Laggy play back on skipping forward or back on video playback. From the Gear VR days this was my go to media player Quest2 and Quest 3. I purchased this program at least twice. I like the default zoom settings the gestures.  As far as I can tell its been quite a while since the application has been updated still on v4.1.3 which was released on October 2023. After numerous meta OS update something seems to have broken in the program to making it unusable for me. In addition to this Pigasus misses out on newer features like Pass through.  Was good till it wasn't.

I bought this to view mpo files from my 3d camera and it doesn't work reliably, sometimes refuses to open perfectly valid files.

It's a shame because the program has a lot of features and could have been great if kept up to date and reliability improved. I now use immerGallery.

When opening a folder containing more than 100 video and photo files on NAS (smb2 or 3), it gets stuck for 10-20 seconds
otherwise works great
works well with my synology nas and plays my movies ok

Whenever I interact with the timeline on the video bar, the videos very often start to stutter heavily. Often, they don't start at all and everything freezes, especially when using the button to switch from one video to another. This issue occurs almost all the time, causing significant problems. I'm forced to restart the app repeatedly to get it to work.

If you will fix the problems let me know replying under my post I will erase my review. I asked  to meta for a refund.

Freezes and crashes constantly, especially when trying to access files on my network, but even when trying to open files that are stored on my headset. Comics files opens sporadically, mostly not. The same with pdf:s.
Used to work a lot better, but now the app is worthless.

I use it for both videos and photos. I actually uploaded much more media to my headset after spending some time with the app because of how crisp photos appear, and videos are a pleasure to watch. The sreenshot feature is a real treat; I use it a lot.

If I made any changes, I would say more options within settings. I won't even nit-pick here. Thats it! Oh and I highly recommend.

reviews.review_title: No longer plays vr180 videos over DLNA
reviews.review_description:
Like the title says it no longer plays vr180 videos over DLNA. This began in one of the 50s updates to the quest 2. I just updated to v65 a couple days ago and the problem is still there.

It will open the video then the video will immediately freeze while the audio continues to play.  I have not tried to play regular videos because I got the quest 2 to watch my 3D movies. I also have not tried to play them locally because the videos are huge and I'm not going to be constantly transferring files to the headset. 

Their support was not very good. They said to reinstall pigasus. That didn't help. Then they told me the problem must be on my end. How could it possibly be on my end? I get the exact same updates that everyone else with a quest 2 gets. That was the end of the support.

Thing is, the videos still play fine over DLNA with the free DeoVR player so the problem is obviously with pigasus.

So if you're wanting something to use with DLNA look elsewhere.

reviews.review_title: This app has never been stable.
reviews.review_description:
I see some reviewers say that Pigasus has gotten worse overtime and it isn't "what it was like before" . 

I just searched Pigasus in my emails to find the order receipt and...

Thank you
Date: May 17, 2022

I'm here to tell you that this media player has never been able to act as a functional VR video player ever. It has never been stable, it has never been usable. Ever. 

What makes this even more strange is that literally every single media player that advertises VR video playing capabilities that oculus has allowed to be listed on the platform including app lab are stable at a level that makes them all seem like they were developed by huge professional development teams backed by a endless stream of top talent. At least when you have this media player to compare them all to. 

When ever a glitch does occur in any other media player and by that I mean all of them. Literally every single one of them. I think to myself "at least this is something that happens once in a blue moon and/or  suddenly a random occurence completely or semicompletely breaks functionality does occur....the problem is rapidly addressed and corrected. You can never expect that to happen with Pigasus.
This is the only thing I wanted VR for.  What am amazing app
From setting each video you own in the exact format you want for it, to veing images in a non-distorted fashion, this app is definitely worth the few bucks they are asking for it right now.

Its a basic media player. Will happily work with dlna server to talk to my devices. Has options for plain/curved screen that can be resized, and options for 180/360 degree viewing. Also has 3d settings and it works very well. Easy to use and cheap at the price I paid. Highly recommend for viewing films off your phone/tablet.
It's better than skybox, I like that you can watch images and videos, and with large videos in a samba directory it don't crash, skybox crashes
A couple of MPO files crash the app. Can I email them to you? Then I can give you the missing star!

developer_response.body: Please feel free to email the mpo files that are causing the app to crash on you to pigasus@hanginghatstudios.com and I'll take a look at them. Thanks.

reviews.review_description: I wanted to view my photo archive of over 70k photos on my Quest 2. They live in Dropbox and synchronise with my Synology NAS. I’ve read a some of the reviews which talk about folder structure not being retained. I can tell you that it all works just fine. Found the NAS, Opened the photos in their directories with minimal loading time.  I’ve been looking for this solution for ages and happy to say this app cracks it. Nice!
